rationale: This reaction shows the decarbonylation of a simple, acyclic aliphatic aldehyde (heptanal) to its corresponding alkane (hexane) using Wilkinson's catalyst. This is a classic textbook example that clearly illustrates the C-C bond cleavage of the formyl group and its replacement with a C-H bond, which is the core transformation of the Tsuji-Wilkinson decarbonylation.
collision_avoidance_note: The substrate is a simple, saturated acyclic aldehyde to avoid overlap with reactions involving aromatic aldehydes (which can be more complex or involved in other named reactions) or acyl halides. The simple alkane product avoids motifs common in other families like ring expansions, decarboxylations (from acids), or deoxygenations (from ketones/alcohols).
